{{Infobox religious building | name = Gongen-dō (権現堂) | image = [[File:Ishigaki torinji gongendo.jpg|280px|Gongen-dō Shrine Hall]] | location = Ishigaki, Okinawa Prefecture, Japan | deity = {{ill|Kumano Gongen|ja|熊野権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Gongen|qq=}} ({{ill|Izanami|ja|伊弉冉尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|1=Izanami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q682306}}, {{ill|Hayatama-no-kami|ja|速玉男尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayatama-no-kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野権現|1=Kumano Gongen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11568925}}, {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|事解男尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野権現}}) | founded = 1614 | main_hall = Three-bay nagare-zukuri (流造) | other_name = Yaeyama Gongen-dō (八重山権現堂) }}
[[File:2015-12-16 Torinji - Buddhist Temple, Ishigaki, Okinawa, Japan 桃林寺本堂 (石垣市)DSCF2126.jpg|thumb|260px|right|Tōrin-ji Main Hall]]
'''Tōrin-ji''' (桃林寺) is a '''Rinzai Myōshinji school''' Buddhist temple located in '''Ishigaki City, Okinawa Prefecture'''. It is the '''oldest Buddhist temple in the Yaeyama Islands'''.<ref>[https://www.yaeyama-nippo.com/2013/08/13/%E6%A1%83%E6%9E%97%E5%AF%BA-%E5%8D%81%E7%8E%8B%E5%9B%B3-%E5%85%AC%E9%96%8B-%E3%81%8D%E3%82%87%E3%81%86%E6%97%A7%E6%9A%A6%E3%81%AE%E4%B8%83%E5%A4%95/ 桃林寺「十王図」公開 きょう旧暦の七夕] 八重山日報、2013rd year 8月13日</ref>.
This article also covers '''Gongen-dō''' (権現堂), the adjacent '''Shinto shrine''' founded in '''1614''', which enshrines the '''Kumano Gongen deities'''.
== History == Tōrin-ji was established in '''1614''' by '''King Shō Nei''' of the [[:en:Ryukyu Kingdom|Ryukyu Kingdom]] on the recommendation of '''Satsuma Domain'''. The temple's first abbot, '''Kan'ō Seidō''', was chosen from '''Enkaku-ji''' in [[:en:Shuri, Okinawa|Shuri]]. He brought '''a bronze Kannon statue''' and '''three sacred mirrors''', which became the enshrined objects of worship for '''Tōrin-ji''' and '''Gongen-dō'''.<ref name="example">{{Cite book|date=2014|title=日本の寺完全名鑑:全国ビジュアルガイド|publisher=廣済堂出版|page=145}}</ref>
According to the '''1713 Ryukyu Kokuyurai-ki''', '''Tōrin-ji''' was a '''branch of Enkaku-ji''', while '''Gongen-dō''' was a '''branch of Naminoue Shrine'''.<ref name="taikei">[[日本歴史地名大系]]（オンライン版）</ref>
=== 18th–19th Century === * '''1736–1737''': Temple expansion, construction of '''Sanmon gate''' and '''Nio guardian statues'''.<ref name="ishigaki_bunkazai" /> * '''1771''': Destroyed in the [[:en:1771 Great Yaeyama Tsunami|1771 Great Yaeyama Tsunami]], later '''rebuilt in 1772'''.<ref name="kuniken" /> * '''1786''': '''Gongen-dō shrine was reconstructed'''. * '''1881–1882''': Major renovations to '''Tōrin-ji main hall''' and '''Gongen-dō'''.
=== 20th–21st Century === * '''1945''': World War II bombing severely damaged Gongen-dō. * '''1947''': Gongen-dō restored. * '''1968''': Tōrin-ji main hall rebuilt. * '''1972, 1984–85''': Major restorations to '''Gongen-dō'''.<ref name="bunkenkyo">[http://www.bunkenkyo.or.jp/archive-site/detail/gonken/ 権現堂 神殿 拝殿 附表門 附石牆]</ref> * '''2006–2008''': Post-typhoon preservation repairs.
== Architecture ==
=== Tōrin-ji Main Hall === * '''Rebuilt in 1968''', following traditional Ryukyuan Buddhist architectural style.
=== Gongen-dō Shrine === * '''Main hall''': Three-bay '''nagare-zukuri''' structure. * '''Unique elements''': Floral carvings, wave motifs, and carved lions, blending '''Ryukyuan and mainland Japanese designs'''.<ref>{{文化遺産オンライン|124464|権現堂 神殿}}</ref>
== Cultural Properties ==
=== '''National Important Cultural Property (Japan)''' === * '''Gongen-dō''' (Designated 1981)<ref>昭和56年6月5日文部省告示第109号</ref> ** '''Main Hall''' (Rebuilt '''1786''') ** '''Worship Hall''' (Rebuilt '''1786 or 1882''') ** '''Main Gate''' ** '''Stone Walls'''
=== '''Okinawa Prefectural Cultural Property''' === * '''Tōrin-ji Nio Statues''' (仁王像) (Made '''1737''') ** Carved from '''Yaeyama Ogatama wood''', '''oldest wooden statues in Okinawa'''.<ref name="ishigaki_bunkazai" />
== Gallery == {{gallery |Ishigaki torinji niou1.jpg|Nio Guardian Statue (Ungyō) |Ishigaki torinji niou2.jpg|Nio Guardian Statue (Agyō) |2015-12-16 Torinji - Buddhist Temple, Ishigaki, Okinawa, Japan 桃林寺山門 (石垣市)DSCF2125.jpg|Sanmon Gate }}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Tōrin-ji (Ishigaki)]][[de:Tōrin-ji (Ishigaki)]][[ja:桃林寺 (石垣市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Tōrin-ji (Ishigaki)]][[de:Tōrin-ji (Ishigaki)]][[ja:桃林寺 (石垣市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Tōrin-ji (Ishigaki)]][[de:Tōrin-ji (Ishigaki)]][[ja:桃林寺 (石垣市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11537060]] Wikidata name: Tōrin-ji [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:12, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox Buddhist temple | name = Tōrin-ji | image = File:2015-12-16 Torinji - Buddhist Temple, Ishigaki, Okinawa, Japan 桃林寺本堂 (石垣市)DSCF2126.jpg | location = [[:en:Ishigaki, Okinawa|Ishigaki, Okinawa]], Ishigaki 285, Okinawa Prefecture | coordinates = {{Coord|24.34361111|N|124.1555556|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map = Japan Okinawa | mountain = Nankaizan (南海山) | affiliation = [[Rinzai school|Rinzai]] sect, Myōshinji school | deity = Kannon Bosatsu (Avalokiteśvara) | founded = [[1614]] | founder = Kan'ō Seidō (鑑翁西堂) | cultural_property = See [[=Cultural Properties]] | religious_affiliation = {{ill|Rinzai school|WD=Q639625}} | location = {{ill|Ishigaki|WD=Q838366}} | founded_by = {{ill|Shō Nei|WD=Q907317}} }}
{{Infobox religious building | name = Gongen-dō (権現堂) | image = [[File:Ishigaki torinji gongendo.jpg|280px|Gongen-dō Shrine Hall]] | location = Ishigaki, Okinawa Prefecture, Japan | deity = {{ill|Kumano Gongen|ja|熊野権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Gongen|qq=}} ({{ill|Izanami|ja|伊弉冉尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|1=Izanami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q682306}}, {{ill|Hayatama-no-kami|ja|速玉男尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayatama-no-kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野権現|1=Kumano Gongen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11568925}}, {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|事解男尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野権現}}) | founded = 1614 | main_hall = Three-bay nagare-zukuri (流造) | other_name = Yaeyama Gongen-dō (八重山権現堂) }}
[[File:2015-12-16 Torinji - Buddhist Temple, Ishigaki, Okinawa, Japan 桃林寺本堂 (石垣市)DSCF2126.jpg|thumb|260px|right|Tōrin-ji Main Hall]]
'''Tōrin-ji''' (桃林寺) is a '''Rinzai Myōshinji school''' Buddhist temple located in '''Ishigaki City, Okinawa Prefecture'''. It is the '''oldest Buddhist temple in the Yaeyama Islands'''.<ref>[https://www.yaeyama-nippo.com/2013/08/13/%E6%A1%83%E6%9E%97%E5%AF%BA-%E5%8D%81%E7%8E%8B%E5%9B%B3-%E5%85%AC%E9%96%8B-%E3%81%8D%E3%82%87%E3%81%86%E6%97%A7%E6%9A%A6%E3%81%AE%E4%B8%83%E5%A4%95/ 桃林寺「十王図」公開 きょう旧暦の七夕] 八重山日報、2013rd year 8月13日</ref>.
This article also covers '''Gongen-dō''' (権現堂), the adjacent '''Shinto shrine''' founded in '''1614''', which enshrines the '''Kumano Gongen deities'''.
== History == Tōrin-ji was established in '''1614''' by '''King Shō Nei''' of the [[:en:Ryukyu Kingdom|Ryukyu Kingdom]] on the recommendation of '''Satsuma Domain'''. The temple's first abbot, '''Kan'ō Seidō''', was chosen from '''Enkaku-ji''' in [[:en:Shuri, Okinawa|Shuri]]. He brought '''a bronze Kannon statue''' and '''three sacred mirrors''', which became the enshrined objects of worship for '''Tōrin-ji''' and '''Gongen-dō'''.<ref name="example">{{Cite book|date=2014|title=日本の寺完全名鑑:全国ビジュアルガイド|publisher=廣済堂出版|page=145}}</ref>
According to the '''1713 Ryukyu Kokuyurai-ki''', '''Tōrin-ji''' was a '''branch of Enkaku-ji''', while '''Gongen-dō''' was a '''branch of Naminoue Shrine'''.<ref name="taikei">[[日本歴史地名大系]]（オンライン版）</ref>
=== 18th–19th Century === * '''1736–1737''': Temple expansion, construction of '''Sanmon gate''' and '''Nio guardian statues'''.<ref name="ishigaki_bunkazai" /> * '''1771''': Destroyed in the [[:en:1771 Great Yaeyama Tsunami|1771 Great Yaeyama Tsunami]], later '''rebuilt in 1772'''.<ref name="kuniken" /> * '''1786''': '''Gongen-dō shrine was reconstructed'''. * '''1881–1882''': Major renovations to '''Tōrin-ji main hall''' and '''Gongen-dō'''.
=== 20th–21st Century === * '''1945''': World War II bombing severely damaged Gongen-dō. * '''1947''': Gongen-dō restored. * '''1968''': Tōrin-ji main hall rebuilt. * '''1972, 1984–85''': Major restorations to '''Gongen-dō'''.<ref name="bunkenkyo">[http://www.bunkenkyo.or.jp/archive-site/detail/gonken/ 権現堂 神殿 拝殿 附表門 附石牆]</ref> * '''2006–2008''': Post-typhoon preservation repairs.
== Architecture ==
=== Tōrin-ji Main Hall === * '''Rebuilt in 1968''', following traditional Ryukyuan Buddhist architectural style.
=== Gongen-dō Shrine === * '''Main hall''': Three-bay '''nagare-zukuri''' structure. * '''Unique elements''': Floral carvings, wave motifs, and carved lions, blending '''Ryukyuan and mainland Japanese designs'''.<ref>{{文化遺産オンライン|124464|権現堂 神殿}}</ref>
== Cultural Properties ==
=== '''National Important Cultural Property (Japan)''' === * '''Gongen-dō''' (Designated 1981)<ref>昭和56年6月5日文部省告示第109号</ref> ** '''Main Hall''' (Rebuilt '''1786''') ** '''Worship Hall''' (Rebuilt '''1786 or 1882''') ** '''Main Gate''' ** '''Stone Walls'''
=== '''Okinawa Prefectural Cultural Property''' === * '''Tōrin-ji Nio Statues''' (仁王像) (Made '''1737''') ** Carved from '''Yaeyama Ogatama wood''', '''oldest wooden statues in Okinawa'''.<ref name="ishigaki_bunkazai" />
== Gallery == {{gallery |Ishigaki torinji niou1.jpg|Nio Guardian Statue (Ungyō) |Ishigaki torinji niou2.jpg|Nio Guardian Statue (Agyō) |2015-12-16 Torinji - Buddhist Temple, Ishigaki, Okinawa, Japan 桃林寺山門 (石垣市)DSCF2125.jpg|Sanmon Gate }}
== References == {{Reflist|2}}
== External Links == * [https://www.tourinji.net/ Official Site] * {{文化遺産オンライン|124464|権現堂 神殿}} * {{文化遺産オンライン|158195|権現堂 拝殿}} * [http://www.bunkenkyo.or.jp/archive-site/detail/gonken/ 権現堂 神殿 拝殿 附表門 附石牆]

=== '''National Important Cultural Property (Japan)''' === * '''Gongen-dō''' (Designated 1981)<ref>昭和56年6月5日文部省告示第109号</ref> ** '''Main Hall''' (Rebuilt '''1786''') ** '''Worship Hall''' (Rebuilt '''1786 or 1882''') ** '''Main Gate''' ** '''Stone Walls'''
=== '''Okinawa Prefectural Cultural Property''' === * '''Tōrin-ji Nio Statues''' (仁王像) (Made '''1737''') ** Carved from '''Yaeyama Ogatama wood''', '''oldest wooden statues in Okinawa'''.<ref name="ishigaki_bunkazai" />

[[File:2015-12-16 Torinji - Buddhist Temple, Ishigaki, Okinawa, Japan 桃林寺本堂 (石垣市)DSCF2126.jpg|thumb|260px|right|Tōrin-ji Main Hall]]
'''Tōrin-ji''' (桃林寺) is a '''Rinzai Myōshinji school''' Buddhist temple located in '''Ishigaki City, Okinawa Prefecture'''. It is the '''oldest Buddhist temple in the Yaeyama Islands'''.<ref>[https://www.yaeyama-nippo.com/2013/08/13/%E6%A1%83%E6%9E%97%E5%AF%BA-%E5%8D%81%E7%8E%8B%E5%9B%B3-%E5%85%AC%E9%96%8B-%E3%81%8D%E3%82%87%E3%81%86%E6%97%A7%E6%9A%A6%E3%81%AE%E4%B8%83%E5%A4%95/ 桃林寺「十王図」公開 きょう旧暦の七夕] 八重山日報、2013rd year 8月13日</ref>.
This article also covers '''Gongen-dō''' (権現堂), the adjacent '''Shinto shrine''' founded in '''1614''', which enshrines the '''Kumano Gongen deities'''.
== History == Tōrin-ji was established in '''1614''' by '''King Shō Nei''' of the [[:en:Ryukyu Kingdom|Ryukyu Kingdom]] on the recommendation of '''Satsuma Domain'''. The temple's first abbot, '''Kan'ō Seidō''', was chosen from '''Enkaku-ji''' in [[:en:Shuri, Okinawa|Shuri]]. He brought '''a bronze Kannon statue''' and '''three sacred mirrors''', which became the enshrined objects of worship for '''Tōrin-ji''' and '''Gongen-dō'''.<ref name="example">{{Cite book|date=2014|title=日本の寺完全名鑑:全国ビジュアルガイド|publisher=廣済堂出版|page=145}}</ref>
According to the '''1713 Ryukyu Kokuyurai-ki''', '''Tōrin-ji''' was a '''branch of Enkaku-ji''', while '''Gongen-dō''' was a '''branch of Naminoue Shrine'''.<ref name="taikei">[[日本歴史地名大系]]（オンライン版）</ref>
=== 18th–19th Century === * '''1736–1737''': Temple expansion, construction of '''Sanmon gate''' and '''Nio guardian statues'''.<ref name="ishigaki_bunkazai" /> * '''1771''': Destroyed in the [[:en:1771 Great Yaeyama Tsunami|1771 Great Yaeyama Tsunami]], later '''rebuilt in 1772'''.<ref name="kuniken" /> * '''1786''': '''Gongen-dō shrine was reconstructed'''. * '''1881–1882''': Major renovations to '''Tōrin-ji main hall''' and '''Gongen-dō'''.
=== 20th–21st Century === * '''1945''': World War II bombing severely damaged Gongen-dō. * '''1947''': Gongen-dō restored. * '''1968''': Tōrin-ji main hall rebuilt. * '''1972, 1984–85''': Major restorations to '''Gongen-dō'''.<ref name="bunkenkyo">[http://www.bunkenkyo.or.jp/archive-site/detail/gonken/ 権現堂 神殿 拝殿 附表門 附石牆]</ref> * '''2006–2008''': Post-typhoon preservation repairs.
== Architecture ==
=== Tōrin-ji Main Hall === * '''Rebuilt in 1968''', following traditional Ryukyuan Buddhist architectural style.
=== Gongen-dō Shrine === * '''Main hall''': Three-bay '''nagare-zukuri''' structure. * '''Unique elements''': Floral carvings, wave motifs, and carved lions, blending '''Ryukyuan and mainland Japanese designs'''.<ref>{{文化遺産オンライン|124464|権現堂 神殿}}</ref>
== Cultural Properties ==
=== '''National Important Cultural Property (Japan)''' === * '''Gongen-dō''' (Designated 1981)<ref>昭和56年6月5日文部省告示第109号</ref> ** '''Main Hall''' (Rebuilt '''1786''') ** '''Worship Hall''' (Rebuilt '''1786 or 1882''') ** '''Main Gate''' ** '''Stone Walls'''
=== '''Okinawa Prefectural Cultural Property''' === * '''Tōrin-ji Nio Statues''' (仁王像) (Made '''1737''') ** Carved from '''Yaeyama Ogatama wood''', '''oldest wooden statues in Okinawa'''.<ref name="ishigaki_bunkazai" />

== History == Tōrin-ji was established in '''1614''' by '''King Shō Nei''' of the [[:en:Ryukyu Kingdom|Ryukyu Kingdom]] on the recommendation of '''Satsuma Domain'''. The temple's first abbot, '''Kan'ō Seidō''', was chosen from '''Enkaku-ji''' in [[:en:Shuri, Okinawa|Shuri]]. He brought '''a bronze Kannon statue''' and '''three sacred mirrors''', which became the enshrined objects of worship for '''Tōrin-ji''' and '''Gongen-dō'''.<ref name="example">{{Cite book|date=2014|title=日本の寺完全名鑑:全国ビジュアルガイド|publisher=廣済堂出版|page=145}}</ref>
According to the '''1713 Ryukyu Kokuyurai-ki''', '''Tōrin-ji''' was a '''branch of Enkaku-ji''', while '''Gongen-dō''' was a '''branch of Naminoue Shrine'''.<ref name="taikei">[[日本歴史地名大系]]（オンライン版）</ref>
=== 18th–19th Century === * '''1736–1737''': Temple expansion, construction of '''Sanmon gate''' and '''Nio guardian statues'''.<ref name="ishigaki_bunkazai" /> * '''1771''': Destroyed in the [[:en:1771 Great Yaeyama Tsunami|1771 Great Yaeyama Tsunami]], later '''rebuilt in 1772'''.<ref name="kuniken" /> * '''1786''': '''Gongen-dō shrine was reconstructed'''. * '''1881–1882''': Major renovations to '''Tōrin-ji main hall''' and '''Gongen-dō'''.
=== 20th–21st Century === * '''1945''': World War II bombing severely damaged Gongen-dō. * '''1947''': Gongen-dō restored. * '''1968''': Tōrin-ji main hall rebuilt. * '''1972, 1984–85''': Major restorations to '''Gongen-dō'''.<ref name="bunkenkyo">[http://www.bunkenkyo.or.jp/archive-site/detail/gonken/ 権現堂 神殿 拝殿 附表門 附石牆]</ref> * '''2006–2008''': Post-typhoon preservation repairs.
== Architecture ==
=== Tōrin-ji Main Hall === * '''Rebuilt in 1968''', following traditional Ryukyuan Buddhist architectural style.
=== Gongen-dō Shrine === * '''Main hall''': Three-bay '''nagare-zukuri''' structure. * '''Unique elements''': Floral carvings, wave motifs, and carved lions, blending '''Ryukyuan and mainland Japanese designs'''.<ref>{{文化遺産オンライン|124464|権現堂 神殿}}</ref>
== Cultural Properties ==
=== '''National Important Cultural Property (Japan)''' === * '''Gongen-dō''' (Designated 1981)<ref>昭和56年6月5日文部省告示第109号</ref> ** '''Main Hall''' (Rebuilt '''1786''') ** '''Worship Hall''' (Rebuilt '''1786 or 1882''') ** '''Main Gate''' ** '''Stone Walls'''
=== '''Okinawa Prefectural Cultural Property''' === * '''Tōrin-ji Nio Statues''' (仁王像) (Made '''1737''') ** Carved from '''Yaeyama Ogatama wood''', '''oldest wooden statues in Okinawa'''.<ref name="ishigaki_bunkazai" />
== Gallery == {{gallery |Ishigaki torinji niou1.jpg|Nio Guardian Statue (Ungyō) |Ishigaki torinji niou2.jpg|Nio Guardian Statue (Agyō) |2015-12-16 Torinji - Buddhist Temple, Ishigaki, Okinawa, Japan 桃林寺山門 (石垣市)DSCF2125.jpg|Sanmon Gate }}

'''Tōrin-ji''' (桃林寺) is a '''Rinzai Myōshinji school''' Buddhist temple located in '''Ishigaki City, Okinawa Prefecture'''. It is the '''oldest Buddhist temple in the Yaeyama Islands'''.<ref>[https://www.yaeyama-nippo.com/2013/08/13/%E6%A1%83%E6%9E%97%E5%AF%BA-%E5%8D%81%E7%8E%8B%E5%9B%B3-%E5%85%AC%E9%96%8B-%E3%81%8D%E3%82%87%E3%81%86%E6%97%A7%E6%9A%A6%E3%81%AE%E4%B8%83%E5%A4%95/ 桃林寺「十王図」公開 きょう旧暦の七夕] 八重山日報、2013rd year 8月13日</ref>.
This article also covers '''Gongen-dō''' (権現堂), the adjacent '''Shinto shrine''' founded in '''1614''', which enshrines the '''Kumano Gongen deities'''.
== History == Tōrin-ji was established in '''1614''' by '''King Shō Nei''' of the [[:en:Ryukyu Kingdom|Ryukyu Kingdom]] on the recommendation of '''Satsuma Domain'''. The temple's first abbot, '''Kan'ō Seidō''', was chosen from '''Enkaku-ji''' in [[:en:Shuri, Okinawa|Shuri]]. He brought '''a bronze Kannon statue''' and '''three sacred mirrors''', which became the enshrined objects of worship for '''Tōrin-ji''' and '''Gongen-dō'''.<ref name="example">{{Cite book|date=2014|title=日本の寺完全名鑑:全国ビジュアルガイド|publisher=廣済堂出版|page=145}}</ref>
According to the '''1713 Ryukyu Kokuyurai-ki''', '''Tōrin-ji''' was a '''branch of Enkaku-ji''', while '''Gongen-dō''' was a '''branch of Naminoue Shrine'''.<ref name="taikei">[[日本歴史地名大系]]（オンライン版）</ref>
=== 18th–19th Century === * '''1736–1737''': Temple expansion, construction of '''Sanmon gate''' and '''Nio guardian statues'''.<ref name="ishigaki_bunkazai" /> * '''1771''': Destroyed in the [[:en:1771 Great Yaeyama Tsunami|1771 Great Yaeyama Tsunami]], later '''rebuilt in 1772'''.<ref name="kuniken" /> * '''1786''': '''Gongen-dō shrine was reconstructed'''. * '''1881–1882''': Major renovations to '''Tōrin-ji main hall''' and '''Gongen-dō'''.
=== 20th–21st Century === * '''1945''': World War II bombing severely damaged Gongen-dō. * '''1947''': Gongen-dō restored. * '''1968''': Tōrin-ji main hall rebuilt. * '''1972, 1984–85''': Major restorations to '''Gongen-dō'''.<ref name="bunkenkyo">[http://www.bunkenkyo.or.jp/archive-site/detail/gonken/ 権現堂 神殿 拝殿 附表門 附石牆]</ref> * '''2006–2008''': Post-typhoon preservation repairs.
== Architecture ==
=== Tōrin-ji Main Hall === * '''Rebuilt in 1968''', following traditional Ryukyuan Buddhist architectural style.
=== Gongen-dō Shrine === * '''Main hall''': Three-bay '''nagare-zukuri''' structure. * '''Unique elements''': Floral carvings, wave motifs, and carved lions, blending '''Ryukyuan and mainland Japanese designs'''.<ref>{{文化遺産オンライン|124464|権現堂 神殿}}</ref>
== Cultural Properties ==
=== '''National Important Cultural Property (Japan)''' === * '''Gongen-dō''' (Designated 1981)<ref>昭和56年6月5日文部省告示第109号</ref> ** '''Main Hall''' (Rebuilt '''1786''') ** '''Worship Hall''' (Rebuilt '''1786 or 1882''') ** '''Main Gate''' ** '''Stone Walls'''
=== '''Okinawa Prefectural Cultural Property''' === * '''Tōrin-ji Nio Statues''' (仁王像) (Made '''1737''') ** Carved from '''Yaeyama Ogatama wood''', '''oldest wooden statues in Okinawa'''.<ref name="ishigaki_bunkazai" />

{{Infobox religious building | name = Gongen-dō (権現堂) | image = [[File:Ishigaki torinji gongendo.jpg|280px|Gongen-dō Shrine Hall]] | location = Ishigaki, Okinawa Prefecture, Japan | deity = {{ill|Kumano Gongen|ja|熊野権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Gongen|qq=}} ({{ill|Izanami|ja|伊弉冉尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|1=Izanami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q682306}}, {{ill|Hayatama-no-kami|ja|速玉男尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayatama-no-kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野権現|1=Kumano Gongen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11568925}}, {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|事解男尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野権現}}) | founded = 1614 | main_hall = Three-bay nagare-zukuri (流造) | other_name = Yaeyama Gongen-dō (八重山権現堂) }}
[[File:2015-12-16 Torinji - Buddhist Temple, Ishigaki, Okinawa, Japan 桃林寺本堂 (石垣市)DSCF2126.jpg|thumb|260px|right|Tōrin-ji Main Hall]]
'''Tōrin-ji''' (桃林寺) is a '''Rinzai Myōshinji school''' Buddhist temple located in '''Ishigaki City, Okinawa Prefecture'''. It is the '''oldest Buddhist temple in the Yaeyama Islands'''.<ref>[https://www.yaeyama-nippo.com/2013/08/13/%E6%A1%83%E6%9E%97%E5%AF%BA-%E5%8D%81%E7%8E%8B%E5%9B%B3-%E5%85%AC%E9%96%8B-%E3%81%8D%E3%82%87%E3%81%86%E6%97%A7%E6%9A%A6%E3%81%AE%E4%B8%83%E5%A4%95/ 桃林寺「十王図」公開 きょう旧暦の七夕] 八重山日報、2013rd year 8月13日</ref>.
This article also covers '''Gongen-dō''' (権現堂), the adjacent '''Shinto shrine''' founded in '''1614''', which enshrines the '''Kumano Gongen deities'''.
== History == Tōrin-ji was established in '''1614''' by '''King Shō Nei''' of the [[:en:Ryukyu Kingdom|Ryukyu Kingdom]] on the recommendation of '''Satsuma Domain'''. The temple's first abbot, '''Kan'ō Seidō''', was chosen from '''Enkaku-ji''' in [[:en:Shuri, Okinawa|Shuri]]. He brought '''a bronze Kannon statue''' and '''three sacred mirrors''', which became the enshrined objects of worship for '''Tōrin-ji''' and '''Gongen-dō'''.<ref name="example">{{Cite book|date=2014|title=日本の寺完全名鑑:全国ビジュアルガイド|publisher=廣済堂出版|page=145}}</ref>
According to the '''1713 Ryukyu Kokuyurai-ki''', '''Tōrin-ji''' was a '''branch of Enkaku-ji''', while '''Gongen-dō''' was a '''branch of Naminoue Shrine'''.<ref name="taikei">[[日本歴史地名大系]]（オンライン版）</ref>
=== 18th–19th Century === * '''1736–1737''': Temple expansion, construction of '''Sanmon gate''' and '''Nio guardian statues'''.<ref name="ishigaki_bunkazai" /> * '''1771''': Destroyed in the [[:en:1771 Great Yaeyama Tsunami|1771 Great Yaeyama Tsunami]], later '''rebuilt in 1772'''.<ref name="kuniken" /> * '''1786''': '''Gongen-dō shrine was reconstructed'''. * '''1881–1882''': Major renovations to '''Tōrin-ji main hall''' and '''Gongen-dō'''.
=== 20th–21st Century === * '''1945''': World War II bombing severely damaged Gongen-dō. * '''1947''': Gongen-dō restored. * '''1968''': Tōrin-ji main hall rebuilt. * '''1972, 1984–85''': Major restorations to '''Gongen-dō'''.<ref name="bunkenkyo">[http://www.bunkenkyo.or.jp/archive-site/detail/gonken/ 権現堂 神殿 拝殿 附表門 附石牆]</ref> * '''2006–2008''': Post-typhoon preservation repairs.
== Architecture ==
=== Tōrin-ji Main Hall === * '''Rebuilt in 1968''', following traditional Ryukyuan Buddhist architectural style.
=== Gongen-dō Shrine === * '''Main hall''': Three-bay '''nagare-zukuri''' structure. * '''Unique elements''': Floral carvings, wave motifs, and carved lions, blending '''Ryukyuan and mainland Japanese designs'''.<ref>{{文化遺産オンライン|124464|権現堂 神殿}}</ref>
== Cultural Properties ==
=== '''National Important Cultural Property (Japan)''' === * '''Gongen-dō''' (Designated 1981)<ref>昭和56年6月5日文部省告示第109号</ref> ** '''Main Hall''' (Rebuilt '''1786''') ** '''Worship Hall''' (Rebuilt '''1786 or 1882''') ** '''Main Gate''' ** '''Stone Walls'''
=== '''Okinawa Prefectural Cultural Property''' === * '''Tōrin-ji Nio Statues''' (仁王像) (Made '''1737''') ** Carved from '''Yaeyama Ogatama wood''', '''oldest wooden statues in Okinawa'''.<ref name="ishigaki_bunkazai" />
== Gallery == {{gallery |Ishigaki torinji niou1.jpg|Nio Guardian Statue (Ungyō) |Ishigaki torinji niou2.jpg|Nio Guardian Statue (Agyō) |2015-12-16 Torinji - Buddhist Temple, Ishigaki, Okinawa, Japan 桃林寺山門 (石垣市)DSCF2125.jpg|Sanmon Gate }}
== References == {{Reflist|2}}

=== Tōrin-ji Main Hall === * '''Rebuilt in 1968''', following traditional Ryukyuan Buddhist architectural style.
=== Gongen-dō Shrine === * '''Main hall''': Three-bay '''nagare-zukuri''' structure. * '''Unique elements''': Floral carvings, wave motifs, and carved lions, blending '''Ryukyuan and mainland Japanese designs'''.<ref>{{文化遺産オンライン|124464|権現堂 神殿}}</ref>
== Cultural Properties ==
=== '''National Important Cultural Property (Japan)''' === * '''Gongen-dō''' (Designated 1981)<ref>昭和56年6月5日文部省告示第109号</ref> ** '''Main Hall''' (Rebuilt '''1786''') ** '''Worship Hall''' (Rebuilt '''1786 or 1882''') ** '''Main Gate''' ** '''Stone Walls'''
=== '''Okinawa Prefectural Cultural Property''' === * '''Tōrin-ji Nio Statues''' (仁王像) (Made '''1737''') ** Carved from '''Yaeyama Ogatama wood''', '''oldest wooden statues in Okinawa'''.<ref name="ishigaki_bunkazai" />
== Gallery == {{gallery |Ishigaki torinji niou1.jpg|Nio Guardian Statue (Ungyō) |Ishigaki torinji niou2.jpg|Nio Guardian Statue (Agyō) |2015-12-16 Torinji - Buddhist Temple, Ishigaki, Okinawa, Japan 桃林寺山門 (石垣市)DSCF2125.jpg|Sanmon Gate }}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Tōrin-ji (Ishigaki)]][[de:Tōrin-ji (Ishigaki)]][[ja:桃林寺 (石垣市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Tōrin-ji (Ishigaki)]][[de:Tōrin-ji (Ishigaki)]][[ja:桃林寺 (石垣市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Tōrin-ji (Ishigaki)]][[de:Tōrin-ji (Ishigaki)]][[ja:桃林寺 (石垣市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11537060]] Wikidata name: Tōrin-ji [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:12, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox Buddhist temple | name = Tōrin-ji | image = File:2015-12-16 Torinji - Buddhist Temple, Ishigaki, Okinawa, Japan 桃林寺本堂 (石垣市)DSCF2126.jpg | location = {{ill|Ishigaki, Okinawa|en|Ishigaki, Okinawa|WD=Q838366|lt=Ishigaki, Okinawa}}, Ishigaki 285, Okinawa Prefecture | coordinates = {{Coord|24.34361111|N|124.1555556|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map = Japan Okinawa | mountain = Nankaizan (南海山) | affiliation = [[Rinzai school|Rinzai]] sect, Myōshinji school | deity = Kannon Bosatsu (Avalokiteśvara) | founded = [[1614]] | founder = Kan'ō Seidō (鑑翁西堂) | cultural_property = See [[=Cultural Properties]] | religious_affiliation = {{ill|Rinzai school|WD=Q639625}} | location = {{ill|Ishigaki|WD=Q838366}} | founded_by = {{ill|Shō Nei|WD=Q907317}} }}
{{Infobox religious building | name = Gongen-dō (権現堂) | image = [[File:Ishigaki torinji gongendo.jpg|280px|Gongen-dō Shrine Hall]] | location = Ishigaki, Okinawa Prefecture, Japan | deity = {{ill|Kumano Gongen|ja|熊野権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Gongen|qq=}} ({{ill|Izanami|ja|伊弉冉尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|1=Izanami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q682306}}, {{ill|Hayatama-no-kami|ja|速玉男尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayatama-no-kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野権現|1=Kumano Gongen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11568925}}, {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|事解男尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野権現}}) | founded = 1614 | main_hall = Three-bay nagare-zukuri (流造) | other_name = Yaeyama Gongen-dō (八重山権現堂) }}
[[File:2015-12-16 Torinji - Buddhist Temple, Ishigaki, Okinawa, Japan 桃林寺本堂 (石垣市)DSCF2126.jpg|thumb|260px|right|Tōrin-ji Main Hall]]
Tōrin-ji (桃林寺) is a Rinzai Myōshinji school Buddhist temple located in Ishigaki City, Okinawa Prefecture. It is the oldest Buddhist temple in the Yaeyama Islands.<ref>[https://www.yaeyama-nippo.com/2013/08/13/%E6%A1%83%E6%9E%97%E5%AF%BA-%E5%8D%81%E7%8E%8B%E5%9B%B3-%E5%85%AC%E9%96%8B-%E3%81%8D%E3%82%87%E3%81%86%E6%97%A7%E6%9A%A6%E3%81%AE%E4%B8%83%E5%A4%95/ 桃林寺「十王図」公開 きょう旧暦の七夕] 八重山日報、2013rd year 8月13日</ref>.
This article also covers Gongen-dō (権現堂), the adjacent Shinto shrine founded in 1614, which enshrines the Kumano Gongen deities.
== History == Tōrin-ji was established in 1614 by King Shō Nei of the {{ill|Ryukyu Kingdom|en|Ryukyu Kingdom|WD=Q28025|lt=Ryukyu Kingdom}} on the recommendation of Satsuma Domain. The temple's first abbot, Kan'ō Seidō, was chosen from Enkaku-ji in {{ill|Shuri, Okinawa|en|Shuri, Okinawa|WD=Q608415|lt=Shuri}}. He brought a bronze Kannon statue and three sacred mirrors, which became the enshrined objects of worship for Tōrin-ji and Gongen-dō.<ref name="example">{{Cite book|date=2014|title=日本の寺完全名鑑:全国ビジュアルガイド|publisher=廣済堂出版|page=145}}</ref>
According to the 1713 Ryukyu Kokuyurai-ki, Tōrin-ji was a branch of Enkaku-ji, while Gongen-dō was a branch of Naminoue Shrine.<ref name="taikei">[[日本歴史地名大系]]（オンライン版）</ref>
=== 18th–19th Century === * 1736–1737: Temple expansion, construction of Sanmon gate and Nio guardian statues.<ref name="ishigaki_bunkazai" /> * 1771: Destroyed in the {{ill|1771 Great Yaeyama Tsunami|en|1771 Great Yaeyama Tsunami|WD=Q4553097|lt=1771 Great Yaeyama Tsunami}}, later rebuilt in 1772.<ref name="kuniken" /> * 1786: Gongen-dō shrine was reconstructed. * 1881–1882: Major renovations to Tōrin-ji main hall and Gongen-dō.
=== 20th–21st Century === * 1945: World War II bombing severely damaged Gongen-dō. * 1947: Gongen-dō restored. * 1968: Tōrin-ji main hall rebuilt. * 1972, 1984–85: Major restorations to Gongen-dō.<ref name="bunkenkyo">[http://www.bunkenkyo.or.jp/archive-site/detail/gonken/ 権現堂 神殿 拝殿 附表門 附石牆]</ref> * 2006–2008: Post-typhoon preservation repairs.
== Architecture ==
=== Tōrin-ji Main Hall === * Rebuilt in 1968, following traditional Ryukyuan Buddhist architectural style.
=== Gongen-dō Shrine === * Main hall: Three-bay nagare-zukuri structure. * Unique elements: Floral carvings, wave motifs, and carved lions, blending Ryukyuan and mainland Japanese designs.<ref>{{文化遺産オンライン|124464|権現堂 神殿}}</ref>
== Cultural Properties ==
=== National Important Cultural Property (Japan) === * Gongen-dō (Designated 1981)<ref>昭和56年6月5日文部省告示第109号</ref> ** Main Hall (Rebuilt 1786) ** Worship Hall (Rebuilt 1786 or 1882) ** Main Gate ** Stone Walls
=== Okinawa Prefectural Cultural Property === * Tōrin-ji Nio Statues (仁王像) (Made 1737) ** Carved from Yaeyama Ogatama wood, oldest wooden statues in Okinawa.<ref name="ishigaki_bunkazai" />
== Gallery == {{gallery |Ishigaki torinji niou1.jpg|Nio Guardian Statue (Ungyō) |Ishigaki torinji niou2.jpg|Nio Guardian Statue (Agyō) |2015-12-16 Torinji - Buddhist Temple, Ishigaki, Okinawa, Japan 桃林寺山門 (石垣市)DSCF2125.jpg|Sanmon Gate }}

He brought a bronze Kannon statue and three sacred mirrors, which became the enshrined objects of worship for Tōrin-ji and Gongen-dō.<ref name="example">{{Cite book|date=2014|title=日本の寺完全名鑑:全国ビジュアルガイド|publisher=廣済堂出版|page=145}}</ref>
According to the 1713 Ryukyu Kokuyurai-ki, Tōrin-ji was a branch of Enkaku-ji, while Gongen-dō was a branch of Naminoue Shrine.<ref name="taikei">[[日本歴史地名大系]]（オンライン版）</ref>
=== 18th–19th Century === * 1736–1737: Temple expansion, construction of Sanmon gate and Nio guardian statues.<ref name="ishigaki_bunkazai" /> * 1771: Destroyed in the {{ill|1771 Great Yaeyama Tsunami|en|1771 Great Yaeyama Tsunami|WD=Q4553097|lt=1771 Great Yaeyama Tsunami}}, later rebuilt in 1772.<ref name="kuniken" /> * 1786: Gongen-dō shrine was reconstructed. * 1881–1882: Major renovations to Tōrin-ji main hall and Gongen-dō.
=== 20th–21st Century === * 1945: World War II bombing severely damaged Gongen-dō. * 1947: Gongen-dō restored. * 1968: Tōrin-ji main hall rebuilt. * 1972, 1984–85: Major restorations to Gongen-dō.<ref name="bunkenkyo">[http://www.bunkenkyo.or.jp/archive-site/detail/gonken/ 権現堂 神殿 拝殿 附表門 附石牆]</ref> * 2006–2008: Post-typhoon preservation repairs.
== Architecture ==
=== Tōrin-ji Main Hall === * Rebuilt in 1968, following traditional Ryukyuan Buddhist architectural style.
=== Gongen-dō Shrine === * Main hall: Three-bay nagare-zukuri structure. * Unique elements: Floral carvings, wave motifs, and carved lions, blending Ryukyuan and mainland Japanese designs.<ref>{{文化遺産オンライン|124464|権現堂 神殿}}</ref>
== Cultural Properties ==
=== National Important Cultural Property (Japan) === * Gongen-dō (Designated 1981)<ref>昭和56年6月5日文部省告示第109号</ref> ** Main Hall (Rebuilt 1786) ** Worship Hall (Rebuilt 1786 or 1882) ** Main Gate ** Stone Walls
=== Okinawa Prefectural Cultural Property === * Tōrin-ji Nio Statues (仁王像) (Made 1737) ** Carved from Yaeyama Ogatama wood, oldest wooden statues in Okinawa.<ref name="ishigaki_bunkazai" />
== Gallery == {{gallery |Ishigaki torinji niou1.jpg|Nio Guardian Statue (Ungyō) |Ishigaki torinji niou2.jpg|Nio Guardian Statue (Agyō) |2015-12-16 Torinji - Buddhist Temple, Ishigaki, Okinawa, Japan 桃林寺山門 (石垣市)DSCF2125.jpg|Sanmon Gate }}

[[File:2015-12-16 Torinji - Buddhist Temple, Ishigaki, Okinawa, Japan 桃林寺本堂 (石垣市)DSCF2126.jpg|thumb|260px|right|Tōrin-ji Main Hall]]
Tōrin-ji (桃林寺) is a Rinzai Myōshinji school Buddhist temple located in Ishigaki City, Okinawa Prefecture. It is the oldest Buddhist temple in the Yaeyama Islands.<ref>[https://www.yaeyama-nippo.com/2013/08/13/%E6%A1%83%E6%9E%97%E5%AF%BA-%E5%8D%81%E7%8E%8B%E5%9B%B3-%E5%85%AC%E9%96%8B-%E3%81%8D%E3%82%87%E3%81%86%E6%97%A7%E6%9A%A6%E3%81%AE%E4%B8%83%E5%A4%95/ 桃林寺「十王図」公開 きょう旧暦の七夕] 八重山日報、2013rd year 8月13日</ref>.
This article also covers Gongen-dō (権現堂), the adjacent Shinto shrine founded in 1614, which enshrines the Kumano Gongen deities.
== History == Tōrin-ji was established in 1614 by King Shō Nei of the {{ill|Ryukyu Kingdom|en|Ryukyu Kingdom|WD=Q28025|lt=Ryukyu Kingdom}} on the recommendation of Satsuma Domain. The temple's first abbot, Kan'ō Seidō, was chosen from Enkaku-ji in {{ill|Shuri, Okinawa|en|Shuri, Okinawa|WD=Q608415|lt=Shuri}}. He brought a bronze Kannon statue and three sacred mirrors, which became the enshrined objects of worship for Tōrin-ji and Gongen-dō.<ref name="example">{{Cite book|date=2014|title=日本の寺完全名鑑:全国ビジュアルガイド|publisher=廣済堂出版|page=145}}</ref>
According to the 1713 Ryukyu Kokuyurai-ki, Tōrin-ji was a branch of Enkaku-ji, while Gongen-dō was a branch of Naminoue Shrine.<ref name="taikei">[[日本歴史地名大系]]（オンライン版）</ref>
=== 18th–19th Century === * 1736–1737: Temple expansion, construction of Sanmon gate and Nio guardian statues.<ref name="ishigaki_bunkazai" /> * 1771: Destroyed in the {{ill|1771 Great Yaeyama Tsunami|en|1771 Great Yaeyama Tsunami|WD=Q4553097|lt=1771 Great Yaeyama Tsunami}}, later rebuilt in 1772.<ref name="kuniken" /> * 1786: Gongen-dō shrine was reconstructed. * 1881–1882: Major renovations to Tōrin-ji main hall and Gongen-dō.
=== 20th–21st Century === * 1945: World War II bombing severely damaged Gongen-dō. * 1947: Gongen-dō restored. * 1968: Tōrin-ji main hall rebuilt. * 1972, 1984–85: Major restorations to Gongen-dō.<ref name="bunkenkyo">[http://www.bunkenkyo.or.jp/archive-site/detail/gonken/ 権現堂 神殿 拝殿 附表門 附石牆]</ref> * 2006–2008: Post-typhoon preservation repairs.
== Architecture ==
=== Tōrin-ji Main Hall === * Rebuilt in 1968, following traditional Ryukyuan Buddhist architectural style.
=== Gongen-dō Shrine === * Main hall: Three-bay nagare-zukuri structure. * Unique elements: Floral carvings, wave motifs, and carved lions, blending Ryukyuan and mainland Japanese designs.<ref>{{文化遺産オンライン|124464|権現堂 神殿}}</ref>
== Cultural Properties ==
=== National Important Cultural Property (Japan) === * Gongen-dō (Designated 1981)<ref>昭和56年6月5日文部省告示第109号</ref> ** Main Hall (Rebuilt 1786) ** Worship Hall (Rebuilt 1786 or 1882) ** Main Gate ** Stone Walls
=== Okinawa Prefectural Cultural Property === * Tōrin-ji Nio Statues (仁王像) (Made 1737) ** Carved from Yaeyama Ogatama wood, oldest wooden statues in Okinawa.<ref name="ishigaki_bunkazai" />
== Gallery == {{gallery |Ishigaki torinji niou1.jpg|Nio Guardian Statue (Ungyō) |Ishigaki torinji niou2.jpg|Nio Guardian Statue (Agyō) |2015-12-16 Torinji - Buddhist Temple, Ishigaki, Okinawa, Japan 桃林寺山門 (石垣市)DSCF2125.jpg|Sanmon Gate }}

Tōrin-ji (桃林寺) is a Rinzai Myōshinji school Buddhist temple located in Ishigaki City, Okinawa Prefecture. It is the oldest Buddhist temple in the Yaeyama Islands.<ref>[https://www.yaeyama-nippo.com/2013/08/13/%E6%A1%83%E6%9E%97%E5%AF%BA-%E5%8D%81%E7%8E%8B%E5%9B%B3-%E5%85%AC%E9%96%8B-%E3%81%8D%E3%82%87%E3%81%86%E6%97%A7%E6%9A%A6%E3%81%AE%E4%B8%83%E5%A4%95/ 桃林寺「十王図」公開 きょう旧暦の七夕] 八重山日報、2013rd year 8月13日</ref>.
This article also covers Gongen-dō (権現堂), the adjacent Shinto shrine founded in 1614, which enshrines the Kumano Gongen deities.
== History == Tōrin-ji was established in 1614 by King Shō Nei of the {{ill|Ryukyu Kingdom|en|Ryukyu Kingdom|WD=Q28025|lt=Ryukyu Kingdom|1=Ryukyu Kingdom}} on the recommendation of Satsuma Domain. The temple's first abbot, Kan'ō Seidō, was chosen from Enkaku-ji in {{ill|Shuri, Okinawa|en|Shuri, Okinawa|WD=Q608415|lt=Shuri|1=Shuri, Okinawa}}. He brought a bronze Kannon statue and three sacred mirrors, which became the enshrined objects of worship for Tōrin-ji and Gongen-dō.<ref name="example">{{Cite book|date=2014|title=日本の寺完全名鑑:全国ビジュアルガイド|publisher=廣済堂出版|page=145}}</ref>
According to the 1713 Ryukyu Kokuyurai-ki, Tōrin-ji was a branch of Enkaku-ji, while Gongen-dō was a branch of Naminoue Shrine.<ref name="taikei">[[日本歴史地名大系]]（オンライン版）</ref>
=== 18th–19th Century === * 1736–1737: Temple expansion, construction of Sanmon gate and Nio guardian statues.<ref name="ishigaki_bunkazai" /> * 1771: Destroyed in the {{ill|1771 Great Yaeyama Tsunami|en|1771 Great Yaeyama Tsunami|WD=Q4553097|lt=1771 Great Yaeyama Tsunami|1=1771 Great Yaeyama Tsunami}}, later rebuilt in 1772.<ref name="kuniken" /> * 1786: Gongen-dō shrine was reconstructed. * 1881–1882: Major renovations to Tōrin-ji main hall and Gongen-dō.
=== 20th–21st Century === * 1945: World War II bombing severely damaged Gongen-dō. * 1947: Gongen-dō restored. * 1968: Tōrin-ji main hall rebuilt. * 1972, 1984–85: Major restorations to Gongen-dō.<ref name="bunkenkyo">[http://www.bunkenkyo.or.jp/archive-site/detail/gonken/ 権現堂 神殿 拝殿 附表門 附石牆]</ref> * 2006–2008: Post-typhoon preservation repairs.
== Architecture ==
=== Tōrin-ji Main Hall === * Rebuilt in 1968, following traditional Ryukyuan Buddhist architectural style.
=== Gongen-dō Shrine === * Main hall: Three-bay nagare-zukuri structure. * Unique elements: Floral carvings, wave motifs, and carved lions, blending Ryukyuan and mainland Japanese designs.<ref>{{文化遺産オンライン|124464|権現堂 神殿}}</ref>
== Cultural Properties ==
=== National Important Cultural Property (Japan) === * Gongen-dō (Designated 1981)<ref>昭和56年6月5日文部省告示第109号</ref> ** Main Hall (Rebuilt 1786) ** Worship Hall (Rebuilt 1786 or 1882) ** Main Gate ** Stone Walls
=== Okinawa Prefectural Cultural Property === * Tōrin-ji Nio Statues (仁王像) (Made 1737) ** Carved from Yaeyama Ogatama wood, oldest wooden statues in Okinawa.<ref name="ishigaki_bunkazai" />
== Gallery == {{gallery |Ishigaki torinji niou1.jpg|Nio Guardian Statue (Ungyō) |Ishigaki torinji niou2.jpg|Nio Guardian Statue (Agyō) |2015-12-16 Torinji - Buddhist Temple, Ishigaki, Okinawa, Japan 桃林寺山門 (石垣市)DSCF2125.jpg|Sanmon Gate }}

{{Infobox religious building | name = Gongen-dō (権現堂) | image = [[File:Ishigaki torinji gongendo.jpg|280px|Gongen-dō Shrine Hall]] | location = Ishigaki, Okinawa Prefecture, Japan | deity = {{ill|Kumano Gongen|ja|熊野権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Gongen|qq=}} ({{ill|Izanami|ja|伊弉冉尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|1=Izanami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q682306}}, {{ill|Hayatama-no-kami|ja|速玉男尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayatama-no-kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野権現|1=Kumano Gongen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11568925}}, {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|事解男尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野権現|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}}) | founded = 1614 | main_hall = Three-bay nagare-zukuri (流造) | other_name = Yaeyama Gongen-dō (八重山権現堂) }}
[[File:2015-12-16 Torinji - Buddhist Temple, Ishigaki, Okinawa, Japan 桃林寺本堂 (石垣市)DSCF2126.jpg|thumb|260px|right|Tōrin-ji Main Hall]]
Tōrin-ji (桃林寺) is a Rinzai Myōshinji school Buddhist temple located in Ishigaki City, Okinawa Prefecture. It is the oldest Buddhist temple in the Yaeyama Islands.<ref>[https://www.yaeyama-nippo.com/2013/08/13/%E6%A1%83%E6%9E%97%E5%AF%BA-%E5%8D%81%E7%8E%8B%E5%9B%B3-%E5%85%AC%E9%96%8B-%E3%81%8D%E3%82%87%E3%81%86%E6%97%A7%E6%9A%A6%E3%81%AE%E4%B8%83%E5%A4%95/ 桃林寺「十王図」公開 きょう旧暦の七夕] 八重山日報、2013rd year 8月13日</ref>.
This article also covers Gongen-dō (権現堂), the adjacent Shinto shrine founded in 1614, which enshrines the Kumano Gongen deities.
== History == Tōrin-ji was established in 1614 by King Shō Nei of the {{ill|Ryukyu Kingdom|en|Ryukyu Kingdom|WD=Q28025|lt=Ryukyu Kingdom|1=Ryukyu Kingdom}} on the recommendation of Satsuma Domain. The temple's first abbot, Kan'ō Seidō, was chosen from Enkaku-ji in {{ill|Shuri, Okinawa|en|Shuri, Okinawa|WD=Q608415|lt=Shuri|1=Shuri, Okinawa}}. He brought a bronze Kannon statue and three sacred mirrors, which became the enshrined objects of worship for Tōrin-ji and Gongen-dō.<ref name="example">{{Cite book|date=2014|title=日本の寺完全名鑑:全国ビジュアルガイド|publisher=廣済堂出版|page=145}}</ref>
According to the 1713 Ryukyu Kokuyurai-ki, Tōrin-ji was a branch of Enkaku-ji, while Gongen-dō was a branch of Naminoue Shrine.<ref name="taikei">[[日本歴史地名大系]]（オンライン版）</ref>
=== 18th–19th Century === * 1736–1737: Temple expansion, construction of Sanmon gate and Nio guardian statues.<ref name="ishigaki_bunkazai" /> * 1771: Destroyed in the {{ill|1771 Great Yaeyama Tsunami|en|1771 Great Yaeyama Tsunami|WD=Q4553097|lt=1771 Great Yaeyama Tsunami|1=1771 Great Yaeyama Tsunami}}, later rebuilt in 1772.<ref name="kuniken" /> * 1786: Gongen-dō shrine was reconstructed. * 1881–1882: Major renovations to Tōrin-ji main hall and Gongen-dō.
=== 20th–21st Century === * 1945: World War II bombing severely damaged Gongen-dō. * 1947: Gongen-dō restored. * 1968: Tōrin-ji main hall rebuilt. * 1972, 1984–85: Major restorations to Gongen-dō.<ref name="bunkenkyo">[http://www.bunkenkyo.or.jp/archive-site/detail/gonken/ 権現堂 神殿 拝殿 附表門 附石牆]</ref> * 2006–2008: Post-typhoon preservation repairs.
== Architecture ==
=== Tōrin-ji Main Hall === * Rebuilt in 1968, following traditional Ryukyuan Buddhist architectural style.
=== Gongen-dō Shrine === * Main hall: Three-bay nagare-zukuri structure. * Unique elements: Floral carvings, wave motifs, and carved lions, blending Ryukyuan and mainland Japanese designs.<ref>{{文化遺産オンライン|124464|権現堂 神殿}}</ref>
== Cultural Properties ==
=== National Important Cultural Property (Japan) === * Gongen-dō (Designated 1981)<ref>昭和56年6月5日文部省告示第109号</ref> ** Main Hall (Rebuilt 1786) ** Worship Hall (Rebuilt 1786 or 1882) ** Main Gate ** Stone Walls
=== Okinawa Prefectural Cultural Property === * Tōrin-ji Nio Statues (仁王像) (Made 1737) ** Carved from Yaeyama Ogatama wood, oldest wooden statues in Okinawa.<ref name="ishigaki_bunkazai" />
== Gallery == {{gallery |Ishigaki torinji niou1.jpg|Nio Guardian Statue (Ungyō) |Ishigaki torinji niou2.jpg|Nio Guardian Statue (Agyō) |2015-12-16 Torinji - Buddhist Temple, Ishigaki, Okinawa, Japan 桃林寺山門 (石垣市)DSCF2125.jpg|Sanmon Gate }}
